prompt: NexusCare Appointment & Billing Management System that includes unified authentication, role-based access, appointment scheduling, and offline financial reconciliation for multi-department service organizations. The Vue.js web interface serves three primary roles: Staff Users (front-desk schedulers and service providers), Reviewers (compliance/finance reviewers), and Administrators (system and organization admins). Users sign in with a locally validated identifier (employee ID, student ID, or email-style username) plus password, then see a role-specific menu with only permitted features and data scope (by site and department). Staff can create appointments in under a minute by selecting client, service type, provider, room/resource, and time; the UI immediately flags conflicts and suggests the next available slot, supports reschedule and cancel with reason capture, and tracks the full lifecycle from requested to confirmed, checked-in, no-show, and completed. A waitlist view allows staff to queue clients by priority and preferred time window, automatically proposing backfills when a slot opens. Back-office screens support user management actions (ban/unban, temporary mute for 24 hours, role changes) and content moderation workflows with bulk actions, a recycle bin, and one-click restore. Financial screens allow staff to post offline payments (cash, check, or locally imported terminal batch records) and assess configurable fees such as a $25.00 no-show fee after a 10-minute grace period, overdue fines at 1.5% per 30 days, and lost/damaged compensation with waiver/write-off approval by a Reviewer. The backend uses Laravel to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js client over the local network, enforcing API-level authorization with RBAC and data-scope checks down to site, device/resource, and work items. Sessions use signed JWT with a 30-minute idle timeout and a 12-hour absolute timeout; passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules, are salted and hashed, and accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts. Recovery is offline-only via admin-assisted reset with identity checks and audit logging; no SMS, email codes, or third-party logins are permitted. MySQL persists users, organizations, roles, permissions, appointments, resources, fee rules, payment and refund orders, ledger entries, and immutable audit trails; sensitive fields such as government IDs are encrypted at rest and partially masked in the UI. Reconciliation is performed by importing locally stored settlement files, verifying file fingerprints to prevent replay, matching against internal orders, and flagging discrepancies for Reviewer resolution with an exception workflow and anomaly alerts when daily variance exceeds $50.00. Consistency is ensured through soft deletes, historical version retention for critical records (minimum 24 months), scheduled incremental sync jobs between sites using diff/fingerprint comparisons, and conflict-merge rules that preserve the latest confirmed appointment state while retaining prior versions for traceability.
frontend_framework: Vue 3 + Vite + Element Plus + Pinia + Vue Router
